item.description:
# Pulse 2.3.0
- Added Screenshots with native macOS full-screen, window, and selection capture, shortcuts, hide-before-capture control, preview actions, drag export, and local text recognition. - Added a richer screenshot editor with pen, text, brush-based mosaic redaction, undo, save, share, pin, cancel, and done actions. - Added Bluetooth device browsing, paired-device connect/disconnect actions, battery and charging-state display, and local low-battery alerts. - Refined pinned screenshot resizing, screenshot preview layout, Clipboard context-copy actions, and app-launch hover handling.

# Pulse 2.2.0
- Expanded support to macOS 15.0 and later. - Kept the macOS 26 and newer clipboard permission status and favorite-removal drag feedback paths unchanged.

# Pulse 2.1.0
- Added the Clipboard module with local history, filtering, search, copy, reveal, delete, clear, direct paste, configurable retention, sensitive masking, and local-only OCR. - Added global shortcuts for waking Clipboard and Applications directly, stored only in local preferences. - Added Favorite Apps, icon/list app browsing, running-state indicators, smoother app launching, and better drag placement in the Applications module. - Refined the Dynamic Island-style surface with smoother open transitions, header actions, multi-display tracking, natural horizontal switching, and Applications as the default expanded module. - Fixed UTF-16 clipboard text decoding when apps provide text without a byte-order mark.

# Pulse 2.0.0
- Pulse now works like a Dynamic Island-style surface at the top of the screen instead of a menu bar icon. - In its resting state, Pulse lightly rotates between memory and CPU, and adds battery status when power is low. - Hover to open the expanded state with Resource Monitor, Settings, updates, pinning, and Quit close at hand. - Alert state briefly highlights low battery, high heat, low storage, or high memory pressure before returning to the resting state. - Added an Applications view for opening local apps from standard application locations. - Improved placement on MacBooks with a camera housing.

# Pulse 1.2.0
- Fixed System language matching for macOS preferred-language setups. - Fixed Appearance > System so open windows refresh immediately after switching back from Light or Dark mode. - Improved the DMG installer window with bilingual drag guidance and fixed Pulse-to-Applications layout.

# Pulse 1.1.4
- Kept the in-panel Update button visibly blue in floating panels. - Added the current app version to the settings footer.

# Pulse 1.1.3
- Made the in-panel update button easier to see and aligned it after the minimal-mode control in pinned panels.

# Pulse 1.1.2
- Moved update checks to the TimeLikeSilver-hosted appcast endpoint. - Refined full-panel status dots and CPU ranking warning colors.

# Pulse 1.1.1
- Adjusted the high-activity threshold for disk I/O. - Reduced Pulse's own refresh overhead without slowing real-time CPU, memory, network, or disk I/O updates.

# Pulse 1.1.0
- Added a pinnable floating panel and compact minimal mode. - Added CPU and memory process rankings with app icons, pixel share charts, and detail popovers. - Refined full-panel status cards with System Runtime and stronger status feedback. - Added Sparkle update checks and in-panel update installation. - Added Appearance and Contact settings, and improved menu bar and floating panel layout.
